mnemonics.title: BOWCAS - Box Or Wrap, Can't Subclass
mnemonics.content: B - Boolean (caches true/false), O - Object based, W - Wrapper classes, C - Can't extend (final), A - Are immutable, S - Support autoboxing/unboxing

mnemonics.title: PANS - Parse And valueOf do Strings
mnemonics.content: P - parseInt() → primitive, A - And, N - Number, S - valueOf() → Number/Wrapper. Remember: parseXxx = primitive, valueOf = wrapper

mnemonics.title: I B F D C - Integer Boolean Float Double Character
mnemonics.content: All 8 wrapper classes in Java: Integer, Byte, Float, Double, Character, Short, Long, Boolean

acronyms.expansion: Java Development Kit - Contains JRE, compiler, and development tools. JDK 1.5 (5.0) introduced autoboxing.
Java Runtime Environment - Required to run Java applications. Contains JVM and library classes.
Java Virtual Machine - Executes bytecode. Handles autoboxing/unboxing at runtime.
acronyms.usage: JVM manages memory for wrapper objects

visualTips.visualization: Think of a 'box' (wrapper) being automatically placed around a primitive value. Primitive int (10) → Box: [Integer] containing 10
Think of 'unboxing' - opening the wrapper box to take out the primitive value inside. [Integer:10] → int: 10
Draw a small box representing cache from -128 to 127. Values in this range share the same object (like identical gift boxes). Values outside create new unique boxes.

analogies.analogy: Primitive values are like naked items (a number). Wrapper classes are like gift boxes that wrap/contain the primitive. Autoboxing is like automatically putting a gift in a box when needed. Unboxing is like opening the box to use the gift.

ArrayList and other collections are like warehouses that only store boxed items (objects), not naked items (primitives). Autoboxing lets you 'ship' primitives directly - the warehouse automatically puts them in boxes.
The Integer cache (-128 to 127) is like a coat check. Standard coat sizes (small/medium/large = common numbers) are pre-hung. Unusual sizes (200+) require new hangers (new objects) to be created.
